skeletons.template:
section .data
    hello_msg db 'Hello, NASM!', 0x0a ; 0x0a is newline
    len equ $ - hello_msg

section .text
    global _start

_start:
    ; sys_write syscall
    mov rax, 1          ; syscall number for write
    mov rdi, 1          ; file descriptor 1 (stdout)
    mov rsi, hello_msg  ; message address
    mov rdx, len        ; message length
    syscall

    ; sys_exit syscall
    mov rax, 60         ; syscall number for exit
    xor rdi, rdi        ; exit code 0
    syscall

; A function that adds two numbers
; Input: rdi = first number, rsi = second number
; Output: rax = sum

section .text
    global _start
    global add_numbers

_start:
    ; Example of calling the function
    mov rdi, 10
    mov rsi, 32
    call add_numbers

    ; rax now holds 42
    ; Exit with the result as the status code
    mov rax, 60
    mov rdi, rax
    syscall

add_numbers:
    push rbp            ; Save the base pointer
    mov rbp, rsp        ; Set up the new stack frame

    mov rax, rdi        ; Move the first argument into rax
    add rax, rsi        ; Add the second argument to rax

    mov rsp, rbp        ; Restore the stack pointer
    pop rbp             ; Restore the base pointer
    ret

section .text
    global _start

_start:
    mov rcx, 5          ; Set loop counter to 5
    mov rax, 0          ; Initialize accumulator

loop_start:
    add rax, rcx        ; Add current counter value to rax
    dec rcx             ; Decrement counter
    jnz loop_start      ; If counter is not zero, jump to loop_start

    ; After loop, rax = 5 + 4 + 3 + 2 + 1 = 15

    ; Exit with the result
    mov rax, 60
    mov rdi, rax
    syscall

section .bss
    buffer resb 100     ; Reserve 100 bytes for input

section .data
    prompt_msg db 'Enter your name: '
    prompt_len equ $ - prompt_msg

section .text
    global _start

_start:
    ; Print prompt
    mov rax, 1
    mov rdi, 1
    mov rsi, prompt_msg
    mov rdx, prompt_len
    syscall

    ; sys_read syscall
    mov rax, 0          ; syscall number for read
    mov rdi, 0          ; file descriptor 0 (stdin)
    mov rsi, buffer     ; buffer to store input
    mov rdx, 100        ; max bytes to read
    syscall

    ; rax now contains number of bytes read
    ; Print the input back to the user
    mov rdx, rax        ; Use the length returned by sys_read
    mov rax, 1
    mov rdi, 1
    mov rsi, buffer
    syscall

    ; Exit
    mov rax, 60
    xor rdi, rdi
    syscall

section .data
    format_str db "The number is %d", 0x0a, 0

section .text
    global main
    extern printf

main:
    push rbp
    mov rbp, rsp

    ; Call printf(format_str, 123)
    mov rdi, format_str ; First argument (format string)
    mov rsi, 123        ; Second argument (the number)
    mov rax, 0          ; No vector registers used
    call printf

    mov rax, 0
    leave
    ret
